seoTitle: How to Add a Payment Method on Shopify Without an LLC
metaDescription: Learn how to add a working payment method on Shopify without a registered business, using personal verification and alternative gateways instead of an LLC.
intro: Not having an LLC or registered business shouldn't stop you from accepting payments on Shopify. This guide covers the alternative gateways and personal verification routes that let individual sellers get a working payment method connected. You'll also see which documents are typically needed and how to avoid the mistakes that cause approval delays.

steps.heading: Understand why Shopify asks for business details
steps.body: Shopify Payments generally expects a registered business, so knowing why that requirement exists helps explain why alternative gateways are the workaround.

steps.heading: Choose a third-party payment gateway
steps.body: Look at providers that accept individual sellers instead of requiring a company structure, such as gateways built for sole proprietors.

steps.heading: Complete personal verification
steps.body: Set up your account using personal verification, providing items like a government ID and bank account information in place of business registration documents.

steps.heading: Avoid common approval delays
steps.body: Double-check the documents and details you submit, since mismatched or incomplete information is a frequent cause of rejected or delayed accounts.

faq.question: Can I really accept payments on Shopify without an LLC?
faq.answer: Yes, by using third-party payment gateways and personal verification instead of Shopify Payments' standard business requirements.

faq.question: What documents do I need without a registered business?
faq.answer: You'll typically still need a government ID and bank account information, even without LLC paperwork.

faq.question: Does this method work outside the United States?
faq.answer: The approach is described as working globally for new entrepreneurs setting up without a registered company.

faq.question: Why does my Shopify payment application get delayed?
faq.answer: Delays usually happen when submitted information doesn't match or documents are incomplete, so double-checking details before applying helps.
